description: Optional standout facts keyed by ISO3. Merged into country facts at load time with category "highlight".

highlights.AUS: Australia is the only continent where all three groups of mammals—monotremes, marsupials, and placentals—live in the wild.
highlights.BHU: Bhutan is one of the only countries in the world that absorbs more CO₂ than it emits.
highlights.BRA: Brazil contains about 60% of the Amazon rainforest—the largest tropical rainforest on Earth.
highlights.CAN: Canada has more lakes than every other country in the world combined.
highlights.CHN: Mandarin has more native speakers than any other language—most of them live in China.
highlights.EGY: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the oldest of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World and the only one still standing.
highlights.IND: India recognizes 22 official languages; hundreds more are spoken across the country.
highlights.ISL: Iceland has no native mosquito species—the cold climate keeps them from establishing populations.
highlights.JPN: Japan has one of the highest densities of vending machines in the world—roughly one for every 23 people.
highlights.KIR: Kiribati is one of the few countries with territory in all four hemispheres.
highlights.LSO: Lesotho is a true enclave—every border it has is with South Africa.
highlights.MCO: Monaco is smaller in area than New York's Central Park.
highlights.MNG: Mongolia is the most sparsely populated fully sovereign country in the world.
highlights.NLD: About a quarter of the Netherlands is below sea level, protected by an extensive system of dikes and pumps.
highlights.NPL: Nepal's flag is the world's only national flag that is not a quadrilateral.
highlights.RUS: Russia spans eleven time zones—more than any other country.
highlights.SAU: Saudi Arabia has no permanent rivers; water comes from desalination, aquifers, and seasonal wadis.
highlights.SGP: Singapore banned most chewing-gum sales in 1992 to keep public spaces clean.
highlights.USA: The United States has no official language at the federal level.
highlights.VAT: Vatican City is the world's smallest independent state by both area and population.
